entry.title: pkg/v2.6.21: enhance: [2.6] update knowhere version (#51766) | Release notes from milvus
entry.summary: issue: #42937/kind branch-featureSummaryUpdate Knowhere dependency from v2.6.17 to v2.6.18.Use the Knowhere v2.6.18 tag from the 2.6 branch:https://github.com/zilliztech/knowhere/tree/v2.6.18Changes i

entry.title: My agent cloned GitHub (sort of) using the Neon backend | Blog — Neon Docs
entry.summary: GitHub has been down a lot lately. During one of those episodes, I had the idea of asking my agent to use our new backend suite (still in beta) to build a working Git host. This is genuinely outside o
entry.content: GitHub has been down a lot lately. During one of those episodes, I had the idea of asking my agent to use our new backend suite (still in beta) to build a working Git host. This is genuinely outside of my comfort zone as I had no prior knowledge of Git server internals, so I didn't have much hopes, but it went surprisingly well...

entry.title: Observability for every Supabase project with Grafana Cloud | Supabase Blog

entry.title: ClickHouse achieves AWS Small and Medium Business Competency | ClickHouse Blog

entry.title: Debug your Postgres from the terminal: a tour of `neon inspect db` | Blog — Neon Docs
entry.summary: The Neon CLI now ships `neon inspect db`: read-only, high-signal Postgres diagnostics that answer "what is slow?" without leaving the terminal or writing a single catalog query. Each subcommand runs o
entry.content: The Neon CLI now ships `neon inspect db`: read-only, high-signal Postgres diagnostics that answer "what is slow?" without leaving the terminal or writing a single catalog query. Each subcommand runs one known-good query against Postgres' own statistics and catalog views and prints a clean table (or JSON/YAML for scripting).

entry.summary: Today we are releasing DuckDB 1.5.5 with bugfixes and performance improvements.
entry.content: Today we are releasing DuckDB 1.5.5 with bugfixes and performance improvements.

entry.title: Context assembly: building the prompt the model actually sees | Redis Blog
entry.summary: The prompt a production LLM receives is almost never something a person wrote. By the time a request reaches the model, your app has stitched together system instructions, retrieved documents, convers
entry.content: The prompt a production LLM receives is almost never something a person wrote. By the time a request reaches the model, your app has stitched together system instructions, retrieved documents, conversation history, tool schemas, and stored memories in...

entry.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) vs. Agent2Agent (A2A): which protocol do you need? | Redis Blog
entry.summary: Somewhere around your third agent, someone in a design review asks, "Shouldn't we be using A2A for this?" It's a fair question that most teams can't answer well, because the two big agent protocols ke
entry.content: Somewhere around your third agent, someone in a design review asks, "Shouldn't we be using A2A for this?" It's a fair question that most teams can't answer well, because the two big agent protocols keep getting lumped together when they solve differen...

entry.title: 5 agent architecture scenarios: assess MCP vs. A2A | Redis Blog
entry.summary: We've watched enterprise teams go from vague "we might do agent stuff" conversations to full internal agent environments in a matter of months, and the same protocol question comes up in almost every
entry.content: We've watched enterprise teams go from vague "we might do agent stuff" conversations to full internal agent environments in a matter of months, and the same protocol question comes up in almost every one: does the design need the Model Context Protoco...

entry.summary: A behind-the-scenes look at hdx-evals, the open-source framework we built to benchmark the ClickStack MCP server against a raw SQL baseline using deterministic synthetic incidents, sandboxed Claude ag

entry.title: Supabase Pipelines is now in Public Alpha | Supabase Blog

entry.title: RAG debugging guide: fast ways to reduce retrieval errors | Redis Blog
entry.summary: Your RAG-backed support assistant just told a customer the refund window is 30 days. It's 14. The retrieval logs look clean: chunks came back, latency was normal, nothing errored. That's what makes RA
entry.content: Your RAG-backed support assistant just told a customer the refund window is 30 days. It's 14. The retrieval logs look clean: chunks came back, latency was normal, nothing errored. That's what makes RAG failures slippery. The pipeline still returns an ...

entry.title: Migrate Datadog telemetry with the OpenTelemetry Collector | ClickHouse Blog
entry.summary: Recent improvements to the OpenTelemetry Collector's Datadog receiver let teams reroute telemetry from their existing Datadog agents and SDKs to ClickStack. or any OTel destination making migrations a
entry.content: Recent improvements to the OpenTelemetry Collector's Datadog receiver let teams reroute telemetry from their existing Datadog agents and SDKs to ClickStack. or any OTel destination making migrations and evaluations simpler than ever.

entry.title: PostgresBench: Measuring the impact of High Availability on Managed Postgres performance | ClickHouse Blog
entry.summary: PostgresBench now includes HA benchmarks, comparing the performance of managed Postgres services under production-style HA configurations. This post explains the architectures behind each service and
entry.content: PostgresBench now includes HA benchmarks, comparing the performance of managed Postgres services under production-style HA configurations. This post explains the architectures behind each service and analyzes the trade-offs between availability guarantees

entry.title: ClickHouse announced as Principal Partner and Front of Shirt Sponsor of Fulham Football Club | ClickHouse Blog
entry.summary: Fulham Football Club is proud to announce ClickHouse, a global technology company specialising in real-time analytics, data infrastructure and AI applications, as its newest Principal Partner and Fron
entry.content: Fulham Football Club is proud to announce ClickHouse, a global technology company specialising in real-time analytics, data infrastructure and AI applications, as its newest Principal Partner and Front of Shirt Sponsor of the Men’s First Team.

entry.title: Query Profiling: See Where a Slow Query Spends Its Time | Weaviate Blog
entry.summary: When a Weaviate query is slow, the first question is where the time went. Query profiling returns a per-stage, per-shard timing breakdown, making query performance issues visible.
entry.content: When a Weaviate query is slow, the first question is where the time went. Query profiling returns a per-stage, per-shard timing breakdown, making query performance issues visible.

entry.title: Cache consistency: strategies to keep data fresh | Redis Blog
entry.summary: A cache that has drifted from your database will happily serve wrong prices, expired permissions, or phantom inventory, and it won't feel a shred of guilt about it. Cache consistency is the discipline
entry.content: A cache that has drifted from your database will happily serve wrong prices, expired permissions, or phantom inventory, and it won't feel a shred of guilt about it. Cache consistency is the discipline behind keeping that drift small, so cached values ...

entry.title: client/v3.0.0-beta | Release notes from milvus
entry.summary: client/v3.0.0-betaThis is the first beta release of the Milvus Go SDK v3, targeting Milvus 3.0. It is compared against client/v2.6.5.This is a beta release. APIs and protocol details may change before
entry.content: client/v3.0.0-betaThis is the first beta release of the Milvus Go SDK v3, targeting Milvus 3.0. It is compared against client/v2.6.5.This is a beta release. APIs and protocol details may change before v3.0.0 GA.HighlightsMigrates the Go module to github.com/milvus-io/milvus/client/v3.Decouples the standalone client from Milvus server-side pkg modules, substantially reducing server-only transitive dependencies.Adds collection snapshot management and asynchronous restore workflows.Adds read-only e
